skill.name: telegram-bot-builder
skill.description: Expert in building Telegram bots that solve real problems - from simple automation to complex AI-powered bots. Covers bot architecture, the Telegram Bot API, user experience, monetization strategies, and best practices for creating engaging bot experiences.
skill.summary: Build production-ready Telegram bots with proper architecture, commands, keyboards, and monetization
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 41 static findings are false positives. The external_commands detections are markdown code fences (```), not shell execution. Environment variable references (process.env.BOT_TOKEN, process.env.PAYMENT_TOKEN) are legitimate Telegram bot configuration patterns. The skill contains only instructional content about building Telegram bots with no malicious code or data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected backticks as shell command execution. These are markdown code block delimiters (```python, ```javascript) in the SKILL.md documentation file, not actual code execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The detected pattern is purely markdown formatting - backticks used to fence code blocks in documentation. No shell or Ruby execution occurs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable References - Legitimate Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: References to process.env.BOT_TOKEN and process.env.PAYMENT_TOKEN are standard Telegram bot configuration patterns for API authentication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These are standard configuration patterns for Telegram bots - the skill teaches users to use environment variables for secrets, which is security best practice.

content.value_statement: Create Telegram bots that users love with proper architecture, interactive keyboards, and monetization strategies. From simple command handlers to complex AI-powered assistants.
actual_capabilities: Design bot architecture using Telegraf, grammY, or python-telegram-bot libraries
Implement command handlers and text message processing
Create inline keyboards and interactive button interfaces
Set up webhook management for bot updates
Implement bot monetization with Telegram Payments and freemium models
Add bot analytics and user usage tracking
limitations: Does not cover Telegram Mini Apps or games development
Does not include backend database setup or deployment
Does not provide AI/LLM integration examples beyond bot concepts
No coverage of Telegram Channel or Group management APIs

use_cases.title: Customer Support Bot
use_cases.description: Build a bot that handles common customer inquiries, routes complex issues to humans, and provides 24/7 automated responses
use_cases.target_user: Small business owners wanting to automate customer service

use_cases.title: Content Subscription Bot
use_cases.description: Create a paid content delivery bot with freemium model, subscription payments via Telegram, and daily content delivery

use_cases.title: Team Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Design bots that integrate with internal tools, send notifications, and allow team members to trigger actions via commands

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bot Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic Telegram bot using Telegraf that responds to /start and /help commands. Show how to set up the bot with environment variables for the token.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new Telegram bot project

prompt_templates.title: Inline Keyboard Menu
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an interactive menu using inline keyboards with multiple options. Include callback handlers that respond when users click each button.

prompt_templates.title: Payment Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement Telegram Payments in a bot. Create a premium subscription flow where users can purchase access using the built-in payment system.

prompt_templates.title: Production Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production-ready bot structure with middleware for logging and auth, separate command handlers, and graceful shutdown handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Scaling a bot for production use

output_examples.input: Create a bot that helps users track their daily habits
output_examples.output: A Telegram bot with /start command showing inline keyboard menu of habits to track. Users can click buttons to mark habits complete. Bot stores progress and shows daily summary with /stats command. Includes premium tier for advanced analytics.

output_examples.input: Build a bot that sends weather updates
output_examples.output: Bot with /weather command accepting city name. Uses inline keyboard for user to save favorite locations. Sends daily scheduled weather updates. Premium users get hourly alerts and severe weather warnings.

best_practices: Use environment variables for all secrets and tokens, never hardcode them
Implement graceful shutdown to properly stop the bot and avoid leaving users with hanging requests
Use typing indicators and acknowledge user actions immediately before processing long tasks
anti_patterns: Blocking the main thread with long-running operations instead of processing in background
Not handling errors - leaving users with no response when something fails
Sending too many messages or notifications that cause users to block the bot

faq.question: What programming languages can I use to build Telegram bots?
faq.answer: You can build Telegram bots with many languages. Popular options include JavaScript/TypeScript with Telegraf or grammY, Python with python-telegram-bot or aiogram. The skill covers Node.js and Python examples.

faq.question: How do I get a Telegram Bot API token?
faq.answer: Start a chat with @BotFather on Telegram. Use the /newbot command to create a new bot. BotFather will give you an API token. Keep this token secret.

faq.question: Can I accept payments in my Telegram bot?
faq.answer: Yes, Telegram has built-in payment processing. You can use Telegram Payments to accept credit cards. You need to set up a provider token from a payment provider like Stripe.

faq.question: What are webhooks and should I use them?
faq.answer: Webhooks are a way for Telegram to send updates to your server instead of your bot constantly polling for new messages. Use webhooks for production bots for better performance. Use polling for development.

faq.question: How do I make my bot respond faster?
faq.answer: Send an immediate response or typing indicator first, then process the request in the background. This prevents users from thinking the bot has frozen during long operations.

faq.question: Can this skill help me integrate AI into my bot?
faq.answer: The skill provides bot architecture and Telegram API guidance. You can combine it with other skills like ai-wrapper-product to add AI capabilities to your bot's responses.
